SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 100041437): O'MEARA, JOHN V. SUBSTITUTED FOR SCHLICHTER, KURT ANDREW AS ATTORNEY FOR H.K. FIRE PROTECTION, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT H.K. FIRE PROTECTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ITS ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF FIDELITY AND DEPOSIT CO. OF MARYLAND (ZURICH) AND THE COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION FILED BY INTERVENOR AMCO INSURANCE COMPANY IS: A) GRANTED AS TO ZURICH'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND B) DENIED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE ALLEGED BY ZURICH AND AMCO AND THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGED BY ZURICH. THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS UNNECESSARY AND DUPLICATIVE OF ZURICH'S OTHER CLAIMS AND THEREFORE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF HK ON ZURICH'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION PER CCP 1061. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORRANO, CSR # 8600 =302/HK

MINI MINUTES:

Defendant, H.K. Fire Protection's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is Off calendar. Duplicate entry. =(302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To 1 Operative Complaint Of Plaintiff Fidelity And Deposit Co Of Maryland As Subrogee To Wells Fargo & Co And 2 Operative Complaint In Intervention Of Intervener Amco Insurance Company
Off Calendar Jul-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Wiliam C. Reeves of the Morales Fierro & Reeves, Tel. No. 1-602-258-0755
appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Jamie L. Keeton of Schichter & Shonack, LLP, Tel. No. 310-643-0111 appearing
for the Defendnt H Fire Protection Inc. by telephone appearance;
Atrt. Robert A. Ring of Ring & Green, Tel. No. 1-310-226-2550 ext. 2 appearing
for the Intervenor, Amco Insurance Co. by telephone appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant H.K. Fire Protection, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternative motion for summary adjudication as to the complaint filed by plaintiff Fidelity and Deposit Co. of Maryland (Zurich) and the complaint in intervention filed by intervenor Amco Insurance Company is: a) granted as to Zurich's cause of action for declaratory relief and b) denied as to the causes of action for negligence alleged by Zurich and Amco and the breach of contract cause of action alleged by Zurich. The declaratory relief cause of action is unnecessary and duplicative of Zurich's other claims and therefore summary adjudication is granted in favor of HK on Zurich's first cause of action per CCP 1061.There are triable disputes whether: a) the leak in the suppression system was in existence by the time HK completed its repair and inspection work on October 19, 2015; b) HK acted negligently in not finding the leak; c) the work undertaken by HK included providing information to the restaurant about the need to conduct monthly inspections; d) there is an implied contract requiring HK to provide information to the restaurant about the need to conduct monthly inspections; and e) HK's failure to find the leak and/or provide information to the restaurant about the need to conduct monthly inspections was a substantial factor in the harm allegedly suffered by the landlord and the restaurant. Liberally construed, the declarations and deposition testimony submitted by Zurich and Amco, particularly from their experts, HK's expert and Mr. Tang, establish a sufficient factual basis for the jury to decide in favor of Zurich and Amco on each of the foregoing issues. The facts submitted by HK tending to show that the restaurant acted negligently go to the issue of whether the restaurant was comparatively negligent and do not support granting summary adjudication in favor of HK on the negligence and contract claims. HK was sufficiently apprised of the issues on this motion by the complaints filed by Zurich and Amco. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torrano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk
